title: cydonia vulgaris var. oblonga
intro: Cydonia vulgaris var. oblonga, also known as the quince, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of quince include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of quince include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and even help manage diabetes and high blood pressure. Quince is often used in herbal preparations, such as jams, preserves, and teas, which can be made by infusing the flowers or fruit in hot water.
